key_facts: Full SaaS suite for solar installers: AI satellite-map design, drone survey, PV yield simulation
B2C viral sharing tools for farmer/homeowner recruitment by installers
Construction management: electronic contracts, warehouse management, work-order tracking
O&M module: real-time monitoring, power generation analytics
100% source-code delivery option offered to enterprise customers
Targets China's 10,000+ small EPC firms with comprehensive digitalization toolkit
Founding team and investment history undiscovered
Not tied to any panel or inverter OEM
business_model_notes: Full-stack installer SaaS platform. Revenue model: freemium (free basic monitoring → paid full suite); enterprise tier with source-code delivery option for large installers who want customization. The free version launch (December 2023) was likely a market capture play — get small installers onto the platform then upsell. B2C viral sharing tools (QR code referral for farmer neighbors) differentiate from pure O&M tools by addressing customer acquisition for the installer. Satellite map design tool (AI-powered) automates the time-consuming preliminary design phase.
competitive_position: Positions as a more comprehensive alternative to SOLARMAN (monitoring-only) or Zhonglai's internal tools. The all-in-one workflow approach targets the fragmented small installer market that cannot afford enterprise software. Direct competitor: Zheguyun's closest competitor is possibly Jinengyun (JinengCloud) for SMB solar management, and PV-master/PV-ware for design tools. The 100% source-code delivery option is unusual — suggests targeting installers who want to white-label the platform.
research_notes: LOW confidence. Website confirmed active. Product features from CSDN blog posts (zheguyun CSDN account, articles from Jan/Feb 2024). No funding data found. Founding year, team, and investors remain unknown — opaque even for Chinese tech companies. The CSDN presence suggests technical community engagement. Product is real and functional based on feature documentation, but commercial scale unknown.
